title: Delphinium inopinum
intro: Delphinium inopinum, also known as Inopinum larkspur, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Delphinium inopinum include delphinidin, a powerful anthocyanin pigment, as well as alkaloids and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Delphinium inopinum is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Delphinium inopinum is classified in the family Ranunculaceae. This classification places it within the order Ranunculales. The genus Delphinium is a group of flowering plants commonly known as larkspurs. Delphinium inopinum is a specific species within this genus. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
